parameters.description: Optional query parameter to specify the number of acquisitions to return.

parameters.description: Optional query parameter to specify how many pages to skip.

parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for an acquisition with a specific identifier.

parameters.name: shipmentTrackingNumber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for acquisitions with a specific shipment tracking number.

parameters.name: isManufacturingAcquisition
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter to specify whether an acquisition is a manufacturing acquisition.
            true means search for manufacturing acquisitions; false means search for non-manufacturing acquisitions.

parameters.name: acquiredFromContactId
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for acquisitions by the acquire contact identifier.

parameters.name: acquiredFromContactExternalId
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for acquisitions by the acquire contact external identifier.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for an acquisition containing the acquisition item with the given identifier.

parameters.name: itemExternalId
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for an acquisition containing the acquisition item with the given external identifier.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

post.summary: Creates a new acquisition.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

requestBody.description:
The request body for supplying the acquisition data.

If the account is a manufacturer (FFL is type 07 or 10), then the caller can create a manufacturing
            acquisition by setting IsManufacturingAcquisition to true in the request body. Note that supplying a value
            for ShipmentTrackingNumber is not supported for manufacturing acquisitions.

To create a non-manufacturing acquisition, set IsManufacturingAcquisition to false in the request body,
            or simply omit it.

parameters.name: listAcquiredItems
parameters.description:
If true, returns an array of the acquired items, as well as the acquisition Id and contact Id. Upon success,
            the response is a 200 OK.

If false or not present in the query string, returns no response body. Upon success, the response
            is a 204 No Content.

parameters.name: listAcquiredItems
parameters.description:
If true, returns an array of the acquired items, as well as the acquisition Id and contact Id. Upon success,
            the response is a 200 OK.

If false or not present in the query string, returns no response body. Upon success, the response

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

requestBody.description:
The request body for supplying the acquisition and acquisition item data.

If the account is a manufacturer (FFL is type 07 or 10), then the caller can create a manufacturing
            acquisition by setting IsManufacturingAcquisition to true in the request body. Note that supplying a value
            for ShipmentTrackingNumber is not supported for manufacturing acquisitions.

Also note that on manufacturing acquisitions, the acquisition contact is automatically set to the
            account contact. Any contact information sent in the request will result in an error.

To create a non-manufacturing acquisition, set IsManufacturingAcquisition to false in the request body,
            or simply omit it.

post.summary: Create a fully-formed pending acquisition in a single request.
post.description:
This is similar to CreateAndCommit, but this endpoint does not commit the acquisition. It remains as pending
            in the database.

The caller must explicitly commit the acquisition to acquire the guns into the bound book.

parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

requestBody.description:
The request body for supplying the acquisition and acquisition item data.

If the account is a manufacturer (FFL is type 07 or 10), then the caller can create a manufacturing
            acquisition by setting IsManufacturingAcquisition to true in the request body. Note that supplying a value
            for ShipmentTrackingNumber is not supported for manufacturing acquisitions.

Also note that on manufacturing acquisitions, the acquisition contact is automatically set to the
            account contact. Any contact information sent in the request will result in an error.

To create a non-manufacturing acquisition, set IsManufacturingAcquisition to false in the request body,
            or simply omit it.

post.summary: Merges two contacts: the "winning" contact and the "losing" contact.
post.description:
Everything from the winning contact is kept. Any entities related to the losing contact are assigned 
            to the winning contact. Finally, the losing contact is deleted.

NOTE: the "losing" contact can NOT be the account owner contact.

post.summary: Commits the specified disposition.
post.description:
If committing a manufacturing disposition succeeds, we return a X-FastBound-PendingAcquisitionUrl
            response header containing the URL of the API endpoint representing the corresponding pending acquisition 
            created during the commit.

If committing a non-manufacturing disposition succeeds and results in a new pending acquisition in
            the disposition contact's account, we return the following headers that point to the newly-created acquisition
            and account:

* X-FastBound-AcquisitionAccount: The account number of the account where the pending acquisition was created.

* X-FastBound-AcquisitionId: The Id (a GUID) of the newly-created pending acquisition.

* X-FastBound-AcquisitionUrl: The web application UI URL of the newly-created pending acquisition.

If committing a non-manufacturing disposition succeeds, we return the following multiple sale report-related,
            headers:

* X-FastBound-MultipleSale: true if committing the disposition generated one or more multiple sale reports; false otherwise.

* X-FastBound-MultipleSaleUrl: If committing the disposition generated one multiple sale report, this contains the web application
            UI URL of the multiple sale report. If committing the disposition generated two or more multiple sale reports, this 
            contains the web application UI URL of the multiple sale reports index page. If no multiple sale reports were generated,
            this header is not returned.

parameters.name: listDisposedItems
parameters.description:
If true, returns an array of the disposed items, as well as the disposition Id and contact Id (if any). 
            Upon success, the response is a 200 OK.

If false or not present in the query string, returns no response body. Upon success, the response
            is a 204 No Content.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the disposition to commit.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

requestBody.description:
If the account is a manufacturer (FFL is type 07 or 10), and if this is a manufacturing disposition,
            then callers may manufacture items by specifying the appropriate changes on the manufacturingChanges object.
            This will dispose the items to the account itself, as well as create a new pending acquisition for the items
            with the manufacturing changes applied to each item.

You must specify a change for at least one of: Manufacturer, Country of Manufacture, Importer, Model,
            Caliber, or Type. For any of these properties that don't have changes specified, existing values will be 
            copied over to the newly manufactured items.

For all other properties on the manufacturingChanges than those listed above, if no changes are 
            specified, existing values will NOT be copied over to the newly manufactured items.

If the manufacturingChanges object is non-null for non-manufacturing dispositions or for requests 
            submitted by accounts that are not type 07 or 10 FFLs, we reject the request.

post.summary: Create and commit a disposition in a single request.
post.description:
If committing a manufacturing disposition succeeds, we return a X-FastBound-PendingAcquisitionUrl
            response header containing the URL of the API endpoint representing the corresponding pending acquisition 
            created during the commit.

If committing a non-manufacturing disposition succeeds and results in a new pending acquisition in
            the disposition contact's account, we return the following headers that point to the newly-created acquisition
            and account:

* X-FastBound-AcquisitionAccount: The account number of the account where the pending acquisition was created.

* X-FastBound-AcquisitionId: The Id (a GUID) of the newly-created pending acquisition.

* X-FastBound-AcquisitionUrl: The web application UI URL of the newly-created pending acquisition.

If committing a non-manufacturing disposition succeeds, we return the following multiple sale report-related,
            headers:

* X-FastBound-MultipleSale: true if committing the disposition generated one or more multiple sale reports; false otherwise.

* X-FastBound-MultipleSaleUrl: If committing the disposition generated one multiple sale report, this contains the web application
            UI URL of the multiple sale report. If committing the disposition generated two or more multiple sale reports, this 
            contains the web application UI URL of the multiple sale reports index page. If no multiple sale reports were generated,
            this header is not returned.

parameters.name: listDisposedItems
parameters.description:
If true, returns an array of the disposed items, as well as the disposition Id and contact Id (if any). 
            Upon success, the response is a 200 OK.

If false or not present in the query string, returns no response body. Upon success, the response
            is a 204 No Content.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

requestBody.description:
The request body for supplying the disposition data.

To create a NFA disposition, set the requestType to NFA. You must also set submissionDate, and type 
            must be one of "NFA/Form 3", "NFA/Form 4", or "NFA/Form 5".

To create a Theft/Loss disposition, set the requestType to TheftLoss. You must also set the
            theftLoss_* properties. The type is implicitly set to "Theft/Loss". Any type sent in the
            request body is ignored.

To create a Destroyed disposition, set the requestType to Destroyed. You must also set the 
            destroyed_* properties. The type is implicitly set to "Destroyed". Any type sent in the
            request body is ignored.

To create a Manufacturing disposition, set the requestType to Manufacturing, and include an object with
            manufacturing changes in the body. The disposition contact is automatically set to the account contact.

Note that the account must be a manufacturer (FFL is type 07 or 10) to create a manufacturing disposition.
            Also, supplying a value for shipmentTrackingNumber is not supported for manufacturing dispositions.

To create a regular disposition (i.e., non-manufacturing, non-NFA, non-Theft/Loss, and non-Destroyed), 
            set requestType to Regular.

204.description: Successfully committed the disposition, and the user passed false or omitted listDisposedItems.

200.description: Successfully committed the disposition, and the user passed true for listDisposedItems.

post.summary: Create a fully-formed pending disposition in a single request.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

requestBody.description:
The request body for supplying the disposition data.

To create a NFA disposition, set the requestType to NFA. You must also set submissionDate, and type 
            must be one of "NFA/Form 3", "NFA/Form 4", or "NFA/Form 5".

To create a Theft/Loss disposition, set the requestType to TheftLoss. You must also set the
            theftLoss_* properties. The type is implicitly set to "Theft/Loss". Any type sent in the
            request body is ignored.

To create a Destroyed disposition, set the requestType to Destroyed. You must also set the 
            destroyed_* properties. The type is implicitly set to "Destroyed". Any type sent in the
            request body is ignored.

To create a Manufacturing disposition, set the requestType to Manufacturing. The disposition contact 
            is automatically set to the account contact.

Unlike CreateAndCommit, you do not specify an object containing the manufacturing changes. You can
            specify those changes when calling the disposition Commit endpoint.

Note that the account must be a manufacturer (FFL is type 07 or 10) to create a manufacturing disposition.
            Also, supplying a value for shipmentTrackingNumber is not supported for manufacturing dispositions.

To create a regular disposition (i.e., non-manufacturing, non-NFA, non-Theft/Loss, and non-Destroyed), 
            set requestType to Regular.

put.summary: Creates a new 4473 and assigns the pending disposition to it.
put.description:
The pending disposition to assign must exist, and it must have at least one item attached to it. The
            disposition cannot already be attached to another 4473.

The disposition cannot be a Theft/Loss, Destroyed, or Manufacturing disposition.

If the disposition has a contact attached, the contact must be an individual, and not a licensee 
            or organization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Id of the disposition to attach to the newly-created 4473.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

put.summary: Creates a new 4473 and attaches the pending disposition to it.
put.description:
The pending disposition to assign must exist, and it must have at least one item attached to it. The
            disposition cannot already be attached to another 4473.

The disposition cannot be a Theft/Loss, Destroyed, or Manufacturing disposition.

If the disposition has a contact attached, the contact must be an individual, and not a licensee 
            or organization.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: The ExternalId of the disposition to attach to the newly-created 4473.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

post.summary: Returns the current ATF Bound Book PDF file for the account.
post.description:
Returns a 200 OK if the current ATF Bound Book is located.

Returns a 204 No Content if the account exists, but has not yet generated an ATF Bound Book PDF.

Returns a 404 Not Found if the account does not exist.

NOTE: this endpoint requires the caller to send an X-AuditUser header.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

get.summary:
Returns the 4473's last printed / previewed PDF file. The 4473 must have been completed by the seller or marked as
Void / No Transfer.
get.description:
NOTE: this endpoint requires the caller to send an X-AuditUser header.

NOTE: if the account uses a third party for electronic storage of 4473s, this endpoint will NOT return anything. 
            They may have edited the 4473 via the third party, so they must download the 4473 directly from the third party.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

put.summary:
Mark a list of items in inventory as verified. Optionally specify a new location, too. You can verify up to
1,000 serials in a single call.
put.description:
By default, FastBound will mark items as verified if there is an exact serial match. Serials with no match
            or multiple matches will not be updated. To override this behavior and only save changes if all serials have an
            exact match, set rollbackPartial to true.

You can optionally update each item's location by setting updateLocation to true and specifying a location.

The verified date must be the UTC date/time. It cannot be more than 15 days in the past or in the future.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

parameters.name: X-AuditUser
parameters.description: Email address of a FastBound user who has access to the specified account for audit purposes.

get.summary: Retrieves the account's items.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for items. When set, other search parameters (e.g., serial) are ignored.

parameters.name: itemNumber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific item number. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: serial
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific serial. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: manufacturer
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific manufacturer. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: importer
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific importer. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific model. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter to search for a specific type. Ignored if search is set. Add multiple to the query string to 
            search by multiple types (e.g., type=Pistol&type=Revolver).

parameters.name: caliber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific caliber. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific location. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: condition
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter to search for a specific condition. Ignored if search is set. Add multiple to the query
            string to search by multiple types (e.g., condition=New&condition=Used).

parameters.name: mpn
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific MPN. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: upc
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific UPC. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: sku
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific SKU. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: isTheftLoss
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter to search for items with a disposition type of Theft/Loss. If true,
            returns items whose Disposition Type was Theft/Loss. If false, returns items whose Disposition Type was not Theft/Loss. If
            not set, does not modify the query. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: isDestroyed
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter to search for items with a disposition type of Destroyed. If true,
            returns items whose Disposition Type was Destroyed. If false, returns items whose Disposition Type was not Destroyed. If
            not set, does not modify the query. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: doNotDispose
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter to search for items marked as Do Not Dispose. If true, returns items marked as Do
            Not Dispose. If false, returns items not marked as Do Not Dispose. If not set, does not modify the query. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: dispositionId
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific disposition. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to filter results by status. Valid values are 1 (Available), 2 (Pending Disposal), 3 (Disposed), and 4 (Deleted).

parameters.name: acquiredOnOrAfter
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to return items acquired on or after this date in the account's time zone. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: acquiredOnOrBefore
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to return items acquired on or before this date in the account's time zone. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: acquirePurchaseOrderNumber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific acquisition purchase order number. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: acquireInvoiceNumber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific acquisition invoice number. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: acquireShipmentTrackingNumber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific acquisition shipment tracking number. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: disposedOnOrAfter
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to return items disposed on or after this date in the account's time zone. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: disposedOnOrBefore
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to return items disposed on or before this date in the account's time zone. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: disposePurchaseOrderNumber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific disposition purchase order number. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: disposeInvoiceNumber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific disposition invoice number. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: disposeShipmentTrackingNumber
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific disposition shipment tracking number. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: hasExternalId
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter to filter results by presence or absence of an ExternalId. true returns only items with an ExternalId; false returns
            only items with no ExternalId. Unset doesn't filter by ExternalId at all.

parameters.name: acquisitionType
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter to search for a specific acquisition type. Ignored if search is set. Add multiple to the query
            string to search by multiple types (e.g., acquisitionType=Purchase&acquisitionType=Consignment).

parameters.name: ttsn
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific TTSN. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: otsn
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to search for a specific OTSN. Ignored if search is set.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to specify the number of items to return.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Optional query parameter to specify how many pages to skip.

parameters.name: accountNum
parameters.description: The assigned account number.

Make it read-only so that AutoMapper doesn't try to map to it. AutoMapper v11 removed the
[IgnoreMap] attribute, and if we reference the property in code, we'll get a compiler warning.

The Id of the "losing" contact. Any related records will be assigned to the 
"winning" contact, and the losing contact will be deleted.

price.description: Optional. Price of an item to dispose.

acquiredFirstOrLast.description: Required. Choose the first matching item or last matching item (by Acquire Date). Default is First.

otherTransfereeEmails.description:
Optional. Other transferee emails to use, in addition to the disposition contact, to try to find a FastBound 
account in which to create a transfer acquisition.

manufacturer.description: Text to append to each item's existing Manufacturer. Must not start with a forward slash ('/').

countryOfManufacture.description: Text to append to each item's existing Country of Manufacture.

importer.description: New Importer value to use on each manufactured item.

model.description: New Model value to use on each manufactured item.

caliber.description: New Caliber value to use on each manufactured item.

type.description: New Type value to use on each manufactured item.

barrelLength.description: New Barrel Length value to use on each manufactured item.

cost.description: New Cost value to use on each manufactured item.

upc.description: New UPC value to use on each manufactured item.

sku.description: New SKU value to use on each manufactured item.

location.description: New Location value to use on each manufactured item.

itemNote.description: New Item Note to add to each manufactured item.

contactExternalId.description:

the Disposition Contact.

NOTE: ignored by CreateAndCommit requests.

If not null, and ContactId is null, the External ID of a FastBound Contact to use as 
the Disposition Contact.

NOTE: ignored by CreateAndCommit requests.

otherTransfereeEmails.description:
Optional. Other transferee emails to use, in addition to the disposition contact, to try to find a FastBound 
account in which to create a transfer acquisition.

fflNumber.description: We're allowing more characters in case the caller sends a formatted FFL number with hyphens.

sotClass.description:
NOTE: this is a string because the default model binder returns the field name and a blank error message
if the caller sends an invalid enum value. At least this way, they'll get a better error message.

businessType.description:
NOTE: this is a string because the default model binder returns the field name and a blank error message
if the caller sends an invalid enum value. At least this way, they'll get a better error message.

id.description: Required. The Id of an item to dispose.

price.description: Optional. The price of the item to dispose.

serials.description: The serials of items in inventory to verify. You can verify up to 1,000 items in a single call.

rollbackPartial.description:
True to discard any changes if one or more serials have no matches or match multiple items; false
to commit changes to serials with exact matches, regardless of whether or not any other serials
had no matches or multiple matches. Default is false.

updateLocation.description: True to update each matching item's Location. False to leave the Location untouched. Default is false.

location.description:
If FastBound.Web.Models.Api.V1.Inventory.BulkVerify.Command.UpdateLocation is true, the Location that Items will be updated to. Ignored if
FastBound.Web.Models.Api.V1.Inventory.BulkVerify.Command.UpdateLocation is false.

verifiedUtc.description:
The UTC date/time of when the items were verified. Cannot be a future date, and cannot be more than
15 days in the past. Default is the current UTC date/time.

disposed.description: True if Detail.Dispose_Date is NOT null; false otherwise.

isTheftLoss.description: If Disposed and DispositionType == Theft/Loss, true. Otherwise, false.

isDestroyed.description: If Disposed and DispositionType == Destroyed, true. Otherwise, false.

totalLength.description:
This property has been there since the API was released and should have been called OverallLength. Because
callers might depend on it, we can't remove it; we can only deprecate it.

items.description: The array of items whose ExternalIds to set.

id.description: The Id of the item to update.

externalId.description: The ExternalId to set for the item. Can be null to clear an item's external id.

records.description: The total number of users matching this query.

name.description:
Required. The Name for the webhook. Max length of 50 characters. Characters must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, -, or _.
Must be unique within the account.

url.description: Required. The destination URL for webhook events. Max length of 255 characters. Must be unique within the account.

description.description: A brief description of the purpose of the webhook. Max length of 100 characters.

events.description:
At least one event required. The list of event names to which to subscribe. Must be a valid event name. Call 
the Webhooks/Events endpoint to get a list of currently-supported event names.
